summary: Iteration 4 — Netflix-themed picks (Intro Overlay, Continue Watching rail, Top-3 awards) verified end-to-end. Backend untouched, 7/7 pytest tests still pass. All NEW testids render and behave correctly. Intro overlay: appears on first visit, dismissible via intro-skip + intro-close, sets localStorage 'deepak-portfolio:intro-seen-v1', does NOT re-appear on reload, footer-replay-intro reopens it. Recruiter profile auto-scrolls to #experience (scrollY=4803, exp top=0 after 3.5s). Engineer profile auto-scrolls to #projects. Explorer dismisses with no flow scroll. Continue Watching: all 6 cards (backend 94%, cloud 88%, data 82%, ai 78%, frontend 80%, quality 90%) render with PROFICIENCY label + percentage + tech chips. watching-prev disabled at start, watching-next disabled at end. Awards: 3 rows with giant 01/02/03 outlined numerals + TOP 3 red badge, content PAT/Collaborator/Hackathon intact. Responsiveness: at 390x844 the 3 profile cards stack vertically (tops 157→380→602), at 820 and 1440 no horizontal page overflow (scrollWidth==clientWidth), Continue Watching rail scroller internally scrolls (sw=1680 vs cw=390 at 390px). All regression testids preserved (hero-*, nav-link-*x5, project-card-*x4, about/whoami/skills/contact-* + contact-signal). Contact form end-to-end submission still returns contact-success banner.
critical_code_review_comments: IntroOverlay does NOT lock body scroll (no document.body.style.overflow='hidden') — it relies on the fixed inset-0 z-100 overlay covering content. Wheel/touch events on the overlay still propagate to the underlying page (you can scroll the page behind it). Functionally fine because the overlay covers everything and auto-scroll only kicks in after dismissal, but a `overflow:hidden` lock would be cleaner UX.

IntroOverlay.choose() sets localStorage with profile id ('recruiter'/'engineer'/'explorer') while skip() sets 'skipped' — inconsistent but both correctly act as the seen flag (any truthy value blocks re-open). OK.

ContinueWatchingRail prev/next buttons are `hidden sm:flex` so on mobile <640px they aren't visible — by design, user scrolls touch. Disabled state correctly toggled on scroll bounds (atStart/atEnd) via onScroll updateBounds.
AwardRow uses WebkitTextStroke for outlined numerals — non-standard but widely supported in evergreen browsers (Chrome/Safari/Firefox/Edge). Visually verified at 1440 and 820px.
Auto-scroll smooth animation for long distances (~4800px at 1440 viewport) takes ~3s — fine for the chained 3.2s setTimeout. No race condition observed.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_FE_<hex6> contact message inserted during contact form regression smoke. Safe to purge by name prefix TEST_FE_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Iteration 4 features verified. localStorage key 'deepak-portfolio:intro-seen-v1' MUST be cleared before testing intro flow. Recruiter/Engineer auto-scroll uses smooth-scroll which takes ~3s for long distances on desktop — wait at least 3.5s before asserting target section in view (top ~0). Continue Watching rail uses internal overflow-x-auto; page-level horizontal overflow check should target document.documentElement, not the rail scroller. At mobile 390px, the prev/next arrow buttons are hidden via Tailwind `hidden sm:flex` — only test arrow disabled states at sm+ viewports.
